title: I/O Contracts, Files, Serialization, Time, and Command-Line Programs · Go Semantics Case: Files Paths Atomic Write
summary: A command must stream records, validate JSON, write atomically, handle time and cancellation, and produce useful exit evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go files paths atomic write increment in a deterministic import and reporting command. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Open files with explicit ownership and permissions, handle paths safely, and design temp-write, sync, rename, and cleanup evidence.
Compose io.Reader and io.Writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
